title: Fe(II/III) colorimetric assay
author: Jian Gong
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bd5fi83n

steps.instruction: Measure absorbance A 1 : A 1 is measured immediately after adding 120 µL of reagent A to 1.2 mL of filtered/filtered-diluted samples or undiluted standard solutions. Mix the mixture well with the pipette by gently pipetting up and down a few times. Load three 200 µL mixtures onto a 96-well plate to make triplicate measurements on the spectrophotometer.

Reduction and measure absorbance A 2 : collect 640 µL of the remaining mixture after step 1, then add 120 µL of reagent B . Mix with the pipette and begin the timer. The solution is allowed to react for 00:10:00 to complete the reduction from Fe(III) to Fe(II). At the 10 min mark, add 40 µL reagent C , mix with the pipette and immediately measure A 2 by loading triplicate 200 µL mixtures on the well plate.

Steps 2 is repeated once only for the standards, from which A 3 is measured and α calculated. In the procedure above α should be close to 0.8. The goal of executing this step is to obtain a realistic α together with estimates of its uncertainty during the assay procedure.
